description: Complete the treb verify command to reach full feature parity with the Go CLI: multi-verifier support, per-verifier shorthand flags, environment-based API key resolution, styled output using Phase 3 formatting, and golden file coverage.

userStories.title: Per-Verifier Shorthand Flags and Verifier List Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want --etherscan, --blockscout, --sourcify boolean CLI flags so that I can ergonomically select which verifiers to use without the --verifier string flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Shorthand flags --etherscan, --blockscout, --sourcify work individually and in combination to select verifiers
No shorthand flags defaults to etherscan via --verifier default; --verifier still works for single selection
Shorthand flags override --verifier when both are specified
verify.rs run() and run_batch() accept &[String] instead of &str for verifier parameter
Existing unit tests in cli_verify.rs updated for new flag parsing
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Multi-Verifier Verification Loop and Aggregate Status
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verification to run against multiple verifiers sequentially and compute an aggregate status so that I can see combined results from etherscan, sourcify, and blockscout in one invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: Single deployment verification against N verifiers runs each sequentially, recording results in deployment.verification.verifiers HashMap with lowercase keys
Aggregate status computed: all VERIFIED -> Verified, all FAILED -> Failed, mixed -> Partial
Registry update_deployment() called once after all verifiers complete; rate limiting delay applied between verifier attempts
--force flag applies to all selected verifiers; etherscan_url and verified_at set from first successful verification
Unit tests covering aggregate status computation: all-pass, all-fail, mixed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Per-Verifier API Key Resolution from Environment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verifier API keys to resolve from standard environment variables (ETHERSCAN_API_KEY, BLOCKSCOUT_API_KEY) so that I can set keys once in .env and verify against multiple providers without CLI flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Etherscan resolves key from ETHERSCAN_API_KEY env var; Blockscout from BLOCKSCOUT_API_KEY; Sourcify requires no key
Explicit --verifier-api-key always takes precedence over env vars for all verifiers
Multi-verifier selection: each verifier gets its own key from its respective env var
VerifyOpts construction sets per-verifier verifier_api_key and etherscan_api_key fields appropriately
Unit tests for env var resolution per verifier type and CLI override precedence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Styled Single Deployment Verification Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want single deployment verification output to use Phase 3 styled formatting so that verification progress and results are visually consistent with the show command.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification start shows stage indicator via print_stage; per-verifier results display styled status (green VERIFIED, red FAILED)
Successful verification shows explorer URL indented under verifier name; failed shows reason
Already-verified skip message uses styled output; all styling gated on color::is_color_enabled()
Completion shows verification_badge summary (e.g., e[V] s[X] b[-])
Non-JSON output goes to stderr for progress, stdout for results
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Styled Batch Verification Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want batch verification output to use Phase 3 styled formatting with a summary table so that I can see all deployment results at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Batch progress shows print_stage per deployment with counter: [1/5] Verifying ContractName (0x1234...abcd)
Summary output uses build_table with styled Status column (green VERIFIED, red FAILED, yellow PARTIAL) and verification_badge column
--all --force output distinguished from regular --all (re-verifying vs verifying)
Non-JSON output: progress to stderr, summary table to stdout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Multi-Verifier JSON Output Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want JSON output to include per-verifier breakdown so that automation tools can parse individual verifier results from treb verify --json.
acceptanceCriteria: VerifyOutputJson gains verifiers field with per-verifier breakdown; VerifierResultJson has status, url, reason fields (camelCase serde)
Single deployment JSON: status is aggregate, verifiers contains per-verifier detail; batch JSON array elements include both
All JSON output uses output::print_json() for deterministic sorted keys
--json suppresses all non-JSON output (no stderr progress leaks)
Schema matches Go CLI treb verify --json format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Golden File Updates for Verify Command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden file tests covering multi-verifier scenarios, per-verifier flags, and styled output so that verify command output is regression-tested.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing golden files updated for new output format (verify_already_verified, verify_all_none_unverified, verify_json_already_verified, etc.)
New golden files added for multi-verifier flags, per-verifier shorthand, and JSON multi-verifier output
All golden file tests pass: cargo test -p treb-cli -- verify
No pre-existing golden file failures introduced
Typecheck passes
